rulings.Case Title: PACIFIC BELL DIRECTORY A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. ROYAL REFERRAL LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion Compelling Responses To Royal Referral, Llc'S Request For Production Of Documents, Set No. One And For Award Of Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 26, 2010, line 1. DEFENDANTS ROYAL REFERRAL LLC, EILEEN BURKE'S Motion Compelling Responses To Royal Referral, L.L.C.'S Request For Production Of Documents, Set No. One And For Award Of Monetary Sanctions is off calendar per moving party. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: CHUNG CHOU CITY, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al VS. GIANTCEUTICAL INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 26, 2010, line 11. PLAINTIFFS CHUNG CHOU CITY (LA#2) INC, CHUNG CHOU CITY, INC. #3, CHUNG CHOU CITY, INC. #4, CHUNG CHOU CITY, INC. #6, CHUNG CHOU CITY, INC. #9'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. Plaintiff shifts the burden and no triable issue of material fact raised. A valid indemnification agreement existed covering the underlying lawsuit obligating defendant to indemnify plaintiff. Plaintff was under no obligation to cooperate in the defense, and was free to conduct its own defense even if there was a duty to defend. C.C. section 2778 (4). Defendant fails to show the settlement was unreasonable. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: GOLDSTEIN, GELLMAN, MELBOSTAD, GIBSON & HARRIS LLP VS. ANDREW EHRENFELD et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 26, 2010, line 5. CROSS DEFENDANT GOLDSTEIN, GELLMAN, MELBOSTAD, GIBSON & HARRIS'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend, no reasonable probability that defect can be cured by amendment. Declaratory relief cause of action appears to allege malpractice against Goldstein which is time barred. Cause of action addresses 2 separate agreements. Cross-complainant's other cause of action adequately addresses claim for indemnity. =(302/CWW/EK)

rulings.Case Title: LEV WEISBACH VS. CCS ARCHITECTURE, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Temporary Restraining Order And Order To Show Cause Re Issue Of Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 26, 2010, line 9. Plaintiff's Order To Show Cause Re Issue Of Preliminary Injunction. Motion granted in part and denied in part. Motion granted as to section 20(c) of the Agreement (covenant not to compete.) Insufficient evidence presented that plaintiff was compensated for goodwill. Plaintiff has shown a likelihood of success on the merits and the balance of equities tips in his favor. Motion denied as to section 20(D) of the Agreement (employee no-hire provision.) The law allows plaintiff to hire employees if they approach him on their own. However, plaintiff has not shown he will suffer irreparable harm if he is not able to hire C.C.S. employees, and the motion is denied on that basis. Plaintiff to post a bond in the amount of $50,000. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: THE JOHN STEWART COMPANY VS. JAWT GREENWALD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Re-Notice Of Pltf'S Motion For Summ Judgment Or In The Alternative Summ Adjudication Against Deft
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 26, 2010, line 12. PLAINTIFF THE JOHN STEWART COMPANY'S Motion For Summary Judgment Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication. Motion for summary judgment is granted. The motion is not premature as it is made more than 5 days after the answer was filed. Plaintiff shifts the burden of proof and no triable issue of material fact raised. Defendant was never added to Goodan's lease that specified he may not assign or sublet the premises. Plaintiff did not accept rent and no tenancy was created between plaintiff and defendant. (Declarations of Stone and defendant.) No additional discovery will alter the fact that defendant has no right to possession of the premises. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID G. MENDEZ VS. NOBLE WILLIAMS
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 26, 2010, line 10. DEFENDANT NOBLE WILLIAMS'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is off calendar. First amended complaint filed on August 12, 2010. =(302/CWW)

Set for hearing on Thursday, August 26, 2010, line 13. PLAINTIFF ROBERT CAPALBO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The court denies C.C.P. 170.6 challenge as untimely per C.C.P. section 170.6 (a)(2). The challenge needed to be made at least 5 days before the hearing and plaintiff's challenge was made only 3 days before. Court continues motion for summary judgment to August 30, 2010. =(302/CWW)
